And it proposes a test that costs zero code and settles it:

> **Take one watchlist name. Open Roni's Ch.13 Excel. Fill in the 4 inputs from Seeking Alpha yourself. Check the margin of safety. If it qualifies and you have conviction — make the trade.**
>
> - If you trade → transparency is the real fix, build the plan.
> - If you trust the Excel but not VIRE → it's VIRE's interface, build the plan.
> - If you *still* don't trade even with Roni's own trusted spreadsheet in front of you → the blocker isn't the tool, and no amount of charts fixes it.

It also flags a structural point matching the other agents: **build order should be inputs first (4 editable cells, cheap), charts later** — and the *charts you're most drawn to are the most expensive and least necessary* for the actual functional job (
